prompt: Pull Meta Platforms' (META) US congressional trades over the last 120 days and chart them by politician — one grouped bar per politician showing reported buy amount vs sell amount side by side — so I can see at a glance which House representatives and senators are most active in META and on which side of the trade.
expectedReason: homepage showcase categorical charts need multi-row comparisons
responsePreview:
Here are the META congressional trades disclosed over the last 120 days (since late February 2026), broken out by politician.

**Note:** The inline visual was not rendered for this turn, so the data is presented below in table form.

**Activity summary**
- **7 trades** were disclosed in the 120-day window.
- **4 were purchases** and **3 were sales**.
- Every trade in this window was reported in the same amount bucket: **$1,001 – $15,000**.

| Politician | Chamber | Buy (Reported) | Sell (Reported) | Trades |
|------------|---------|----------------|-----------------|--------|
| John McGuire | House | $1,001 – $15,000 | — | 1 |
| Julia Letlow | House | $1,001 – $15,000 | — | 1 |
| Ro Khanna | House | $1,001 – $15,000 | $1,001 – $15,000 | 2 |
| Michael McCaul | House | $1,001 – $15,000 | — | 1 |
| Angus King | Senate | — | $1,001 – $15,000 | 1 |
| Matt Van Epps | House | — | $1,001 – $15,000 | 1 |

**What the data shows**
- **Ro Khanna** was the only politician with activity on both sides (one buy and one sell).
- **Michael McCaul, John McGuire, and Julia Letlow** each reported a single purchase.
- **Angus King** (Senate) and **Matt Van Epps** each reported a single sale.
